meta.description:
# Sigstore Signature Verification (dual-style)

Passes when a release artifact carries a verified sigstore signature
from an accepted signer, regardless of whether it was signed the
legacy way (a detached Fulcio certificate + signature pair, e.g.
`<artifact>.pem` + `<artifact>.sig`) or with a modern sigstore
bundle (`<artifact>.sigstore.json` / `.bundle`).

The carabiner collector normalizes both signing styles into a single
signature attestation (`https://carabiner.dev/ampel/signature/v1`):
an empty predicate whose only payload is the verified signer
identity. AMPEL matches that signer against the identities supplied
at verification time (`--signer`) or declared in a policy
`identities` block, and only admits attestations from an accepted
signer. This tenet then asserts that at least one such attestation is
present **and** that its matched signer identity is set and is a
sigstore identity — so a signer must be pinned (an empty match, or a
key/reference identity, is rejected). A single policy therefore
applies to any release no matter how it was signed.

## Verifying

The signer identity is enforced by AMPEL, not by the tenet code, so
supply the expected release identity with `--signer`:

```
ampel verify <artifact> \
    -c release:github.com/ORG/REPO@vX.Y.Z \
    -p git+https://github.com/carabiner-dev/policies#sigstore/sigstore-dual-signatures.hjson \
    --signer 'sigstore::https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com::https://github.com/ORG/REPO/.github/workflows/release.yml@refs/tags/vX.Y.Z'
```

Alternatively, pin the accepted signer(s) directly in the policy by
adding an `identities` block (sigstore `mode: regexp` lets a single
entry match a release workflow across every tag).

assessment.message: A verified sigstore signature from an accepted sigstore signer is present

error.guidance: The release must carry a sigstore signature (detached cert + sig, or a sigstore bundle) whose signer matches an accepted sigstore identity. Pass the expected identity with --signer 'sigstore::<issuer>::<identity>' (or declare it in the policy) — a key or reference identity, or no identity at all, is rejected.
error.message: No verified sigstore signature from an accepted sigstore signer
